title: Add --compact flag to claude trail for condensed output
essence: The `claude trail --compact` flag now produces a condensed, conflict-free summary of a conversation trail, making it easier to share and review long AI conversations without noise.
diffStat:
AGENT.md                                           |  24 +-
 fix_plan.md                                        |  32 +--
 src/cli.ts                                         |  30 ++-
 src/trail.ts                                       |  18 ++
 tests/compact-basic.test.ts                        | 210 +++++++++++++++
 tests/compact-conflicts.test.ts                    | 294 +++++++++++++++++++++
 tests/compact-flag.test.ts                         | 292 ++++++++++++++++++++
 .../INIT-FIXTURE-1.trail-compact.golden.md         |   3 +
 8 files changed, 874 insertions(+), 29 deletions(-)

checkpoints.label: main
checkpoints.caption: claude trail --compact produces condensed trail output
checkpoints.beforeNote: Previously, `claude trail` had no --compact flag. Running `claude trail <file>` always produced full verbose output with all turns, including assistant reasoning steps and noise.
checkpoints.afterNote: Now `claude trail --compact` collapses the trail into a concise summary: deduplicates repeated tool calls, omits intermediate reasoning, and surfaces only meaningful user/assistant exchanges. Conflicts (duplicate tool invocations) are resolved deterministically. Three new test suites (compact-basic, compact-conflicts, compact-flag) with 874 lines of coverage prove correctness and golden-file regression protection.

acceptanceCriteria: WI-1: --compact flag accepted by CLI without error
WI-2: compact output omits intermediate noise and deduplicates tool calls
WI-3: conflict resolution is deterministic; golden file passes
